Title: Nocturne
Age: Current999 – 1002 years old Timeline Born6 BN – 3 BN Edit

children.title: Origin
children.textContent:
Nocturne was inadvertently created during the tail end of the Rune Wars. Escalating tensions led desperate mages to resort to shadow magic, casting off their flesh and stepping into the spirit realm. Shadow mages became so powerful and pervasive that Nocturne was brought to life within the spirit realm, though the exact details of its manifestation remain a mystery.

Nocturne preyed on the mages who entered its domain so thoroughly and brutally that shadow magic become known as a dangerous and forbidden art. As the number of shadow mages dwindled, Nocturne languished in the spirit realm, starving for centuries. Eventually, however, Nocturne found the means to materialize in the physical world, and began to prey on mortal fear wherever it could.

Some time after the Rune Wars had ended, Nocturne tormented the nightmares of a Demacian historian named Jens Tommen, who witnessed unimaginable horrors during his studies of the Shadow Isles. Feeling he might lose himself to paranoia, Tommen purchased transport to the Demacian settlement of Fossbarrow, where he hoped his nightmares would finally end.

children.title: Haunting of Fossbarrow
children.textContent:
According to local legend, a nightmarish demon once attacked the Demacian settlement of Fossbarrow, where it did battle against Fossian Crownguard, the famed hero for whom the town was originally named. As the story tells, Fossian and the demon fell from the top of the cliff, where he plunged his Demacian steel blade into the creature's black heart and ended its life once and for all. Fossian was mortally wounded after the fall, but the people honored him with a tomb in a cave at the side of the mountain, and celebrated his achievement in the form of a carving upon a marble slab.

Unbeknownst to anyone else, Nocturne was still alive, sealed and sleeping in Fossian's tomb as it gathered its strength once more. After many years, Nocturne sensed the nightmares of Luca, a young mage who was the son of the local magistrate, fearful of his own growing magic and the hatred of his own neighbors. To Nocturne, these fears were childish and lacking any real substance, yet they proved potent enough to wake the demon from its long slumber. It began to stoke the nightmares within Luca's mind, tormenting him until he eventually made his way to Fossian's tomb, wanting to be near someone who had slain a demon. Here, Nocturne made Luca sleep and fully possessed his body. Luca was soon declared missing, and darkness soon began to spread into the surrounding forests and the city itself, infesting the dreams of every person in Fossbarrow and leaving them hopeless, sleepless, and paranoid in the following days.

Fossian's great-granddaughter Luxanna Crownguard arrived in Fossbarrow shortly after Luca's disappearance. She told the guards she had come to honor her fallen forebear, but in truth, she wanted to explore the forests outside the city and learn more about her own magical abilities. On her first night there, Nocturne tormented her with a dream where she was sinking and suffocating in dark mud, with her light being snuffed out forever. While she was out riding the next morning, Nocturne pursued Lux again, urging her to sleep while she was investigating the town's curse and Luca's disappearance. Nocturne's attempt to subdue Lux was unsuccessful, as Garen and four other Dauntless Vanguard found her and interrupted the 2 darkness from taking hold.

As Garen and Lux were searching for Luca, Nocturne used the paranoia of Fossbarrow's townsfolk to turn them against each other, driving a man named Aldo Dayan mad and making him murder his entire family and several of his neighbors. When he was caught, the townsfolk became riled up in a mob and demanded his execution, which was carried out by Garen after Magistrate Giselle gave the order. After Dayan was beheaded, the curse revealed itself and the whole town became flooded with the manifestations of all the inhabitants' worst nightmares, which took the form of monstrous visions and 4 shadows. Lux, having realized the source of the terror, rode to face Nocturne as Garen and his soldiers held off the creatures' advance.

Upon entering Fossian's tomb, Lux found the young Luca, still possessed by the demon. When Lux tried to approach, Nocturne immobilized her by simply wagging a finger, then prepared to crack her head open with a sharp rock so it could feast on her nightmares. As Lux attempted to get through to Luca, Nocturne gloated that the boy could no longer hear her, and that even if he could, his terror was far too powerful to break free from. Nocturne believed Lux should know this all too well, for she was also a mage hiding from her own family and countrymen. In a swift act of defiance, Lux acknowledged her fear and refused to let it control her, building enough of a spark to move and make contact with Luca, expelling the demon with her magic. Nocturne, now loose in the cave, was subsequently defeated by the combined magic of Lux and Luca, but Lux admitted that even though the "nightmare-man" had been banished from the tomb and Fossbarrow, it was likely not gone for good.

children.title: Encounter with Sylas
children.textContent: During the Great Mage Rebellion, Nocturne continued to feed on the nightmares of the Demacians, lurking in the forests and waiting for a chance to strike at unsuspecting travelers. One day, as Sylas and another rebel named Yops were passing through in search of mages to join their cause, Nocturne began to stalk them, feeding on their fears. They soon became aware they were being pursued and fought Nocturne, forcing the demon to flee.

children.title: The Shadow Door
children.textContent: One night in Piltover, Nocturne haunted the dreams of a man named Celwyn, tormenting him with visions of his son Abel being eaten alive after he read him a bedtime story. The nightmare persisted even after Celwyn thought he had already woken up, until Nocturne revealed itself to Celwyn and plunged its wicked blades into his heart. The next morning, Celwyn was nowhere to be found, the only trace of him being a few tendrils of dark mist. Abel, unable to find his father, left the room. The mist began to follow him.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent:
Nocturne is made of pure, inky-black darkness, only vaguely humanoid with a head and two arms. Its fingers appear clawed and its head is featureless save for two solid white eyes. From the waist down, its body seems to dissolve into a plume of black smoke.

It is usually seen wearing armor, either purple or red with a gray trim. It wears a breastplate and two large, spiked pauldrons. Its signature weapons are a pair of massive blades attached to either forearm, both viciously curved with numerous points.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Nocturne is a demonic entity that thrives on the nourishment derived from fear induced by nightmares. This malevolent being possesses an intimate understanding of individuals' deepest fears, allowing it to expertly exploit and manipulate their vulnerabilities. In its pursuit of sustenance, Nocturne revels in the psychological torment it inflicts upon its victims, creating an atmosphere of profound dread and isolation.

In its insatiable quest for mortal fear, Nocturne revels in the perverse satisfaction derived from plunging the world into shadows. This malefic entity holds an innate disdain for the brilliance of light and seeks to envelop every soul it encounters in an eternal abyss of darkness. In this relentless pursuit, Nocturne spares no mercy for the innocent and remorselessly extinguishes life, all while feeding voraciously on the exquisite banquet of nightmares and the paralyzing grip of terror.

children.title: Sylas
children.textContent: Sylas used to hear tales about Nocturne when he was young. During the Great Mage Rebellion, Sylas encountered Nocturne in the woods, disguising itself as Morgana and admonishing him for the blood on his hands. Seeing through the demon's illusion, Sylas defeated Nocturne in battle, reverting to its true form and fading away.
